raw_consumer_statement: After filing Consumer Financial Protection Bureau complaint XXXX on XX/XX/year> I was notified by citi that my {$170.00} conditional credit was now permanent, and that there was nothing else I needed to do. On XX/XX/year> I was notified that my conditional credit of {$260.00} had been reversed. {$260.00} was the original dispute amount, with the merchant shipping a partial order of $ XXXX $ XXXX {$93.00}. citi cites the merchant providing an invoice showing the items disputed as proof of delivery. The reason for the dispute is that I did not recieve the items on the invoice, rather the contents were what I did not order, and were in fact for another customer. Attempts to resolve this issue with the vendor had gone unanswered, thus the dispute filed with citi. The scan of the invoice in the shipment, as well as photo of the invoice with package contents clearly show that I did not obtain the items that I ordered and the vendor has made an error. I can not understand why citi sides with the vendor and need assistance with a permanent resolution to this issue.
